description_long: This is a sleek, white military jet aircraft marked with 'U.S. AIR FORCE' insignia and roundels on its fuselage and wings. The airplane features a sharply pointed nose, a single vertical stabilizer, and a delta wing configuration that gives it a triangular planform. The cockpit is positioned at the front of the fuselage and is enclosed with a dark canopy. The landing gear is visible and appears to be of the tricycle type, with one nose wheel and two main wheels. The surface is smooth and metallic, with minimal visible panel lines and a few blue accent stripes on the tail.
description: A white, delta-wing U.S. Air Force jet with a pointed nose, single vertical stabilizer, and tricycle landing gear. The cockpit is forward and enclosed with a dark canopy.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the jet, U.S. AIR FORCE markings, and the main landing gear extended.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the sharp nose, delta wing shape, and the narrow profile of the cockpit canopy.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with similar features to view 0, showing the tail and wing markings more clearly.
description_view_3: Top-down view emphasizing the triangular delta wing planform and the placement of the cockpit and tail.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, delta wing configuration with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers at the base.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle, retractable landing gear with one nose wheel and two main gear units.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a single-seat canopy integrated into the fuselage.
